title: Marital infidelity, abortion, PCP2

audience_match: Catholic clergy, religious, and lay leaders
Members of the Philippine bench and bar

primary_topics: Reader-prompted continuation of the prior week's 'rocky road to liberalism' column with a genuinely rocky example
XXX v People (April 16, 2024, J Hernando, en banc, 9-5-1) â€” marital infidelity as psychological violence under RA 9262
Asymmetry: RA 9262 punishes only philandering husbands; sub-silentio on philandering wives
Imbong v Ochoa (April 9, 2014, J Mendoza, en banc, 11-1-3) â€” life begins at fertilization; Lagman's implantation theory rejected
PCP2 Article 28 â€” laity must form civic conscience; clergy must refrain from partisan politics
sub_topics: Section 5(i) of RA 9262 â€” mental/emotional anguish, public ridicule, denial of support
Majority holding: criminal intent to cause anguish may be conclusively presumed from the fact of infidelity itself
Minority position: intent not proven; awaiting a new opportunity when Court composition changes
Justices Lazaro-Javier and Maria Filomena D. Singh as concurring lady justices
CJ's 'simple advice to husbands': infidelity is dangerous to life and liberty
RA 9262 silence on wives â€” a noted gender asymmetry
Abortion as live issue in US presidential campaign; in PH abortion is barred by Constitution and penalized
Imbong v Ochoa â€” when does life begin: at fertilization (sperm-egg union)
Constitutional-deliberations record â€” Bernardo M. Villegas and (later CJ) Hilario G. Davide Jr. on protection 'from the moment of conception'
Right to life as grounded in natural law, inherent, not creation of statute
Consequence: health professionals cannot be compelled to use post-fertilization devices/drugs; only 'rhythm method' for some theologians
PCP2 Article 28 #1 â€” lay men and women must help form civic conscience and promote leaders of true integrity
PCP2 Article 28 #2 â€” bishops, priests, religious must refrain from partisan politics; Canons 287 #2 and 672
Book reference: 'Acts and Decrees of the Second Plenary Council of the Philippines,' P308 paperback at St. Paul's Superstore, San Antonio Village, Makati

signature_phrases.phrase: A reader observed
the road to liberalism of our Supreme Court and the Catholic Church is rocky
What a keen observation
Let me now exemplify a rocky one
XXX vs People
punishable with imprisonment and fine
Causing mental or emotional anguish, public ridicule or humiliation
An illicit sexual encounter committed by a male person, however casual or infrequent, constitutes marital infidelity that is tantamount to psychological violence
the criminal intent of the husband to commit mental or emotional anguish via his infidelity had not been proven
the specific intent to cause mental and emotional anguish upon the victim may be conclusively presumed from the fact of infidelity itself
Incumbent lady Justices Amy C. Lazaro-Javier and Maria Filomena D. Singh
wrote compelling concurring opinions
the minority of five is looking for another case with similar facts for a new opportunity to prevail when the composition of the Court changes
my simple advice to husbands is: Infidelity, though casual or occasional, and whether with a willing mistress or a one-night stand, is dangerous to life and liberty
How about women betraying their husbands? Sorry, RA 9262 punishes only philandering husbands; it is sub-silentio on philandering wives
THE ALLEGED RIGHT TO ABORTION
in the Philippines, such battle is passÃ©
Abortion is barred by our Constitution and penalized by our laws as a crime
When does life begin
the landmark 106-page Imbong vs Ochoa
a zygote is a human organism
the life of a new human being commences at a scientifically well-defined moment of conception, that is, upon fertilization
the Court cannot subscribe to the theory advocated by Hon. [Edcel] Lagman that life begins at implantation
stressed during the constitutional deliberations that life must be protected 'from the moment of conception'
every human being enjoys the right to life
Even if not formally established, the right to life, being grounded on natural law, is inherent
health professionals cannot be forced to use any device, method, or drug that prevents the fertilized ovum from reaching the maternal womb
Some theologians say that only the 'rhythm method' should be prescribed in family planning
THE VATICAN II AND PCP2 DOCUMENTS are quite voluminous and complicated
I do not have the expertise in canon law or the space to dwell on them
Lay men and women in responsible position in our society must help form the civic conscience
Bishops, priests and religious must refrain from partisan politics, avoiding especially the use of the pulpit for partisan purposes
Those with further questions may consult a canon lawyer or purchase the book for P308

people: Ramon Paul L. Hernando (Justice; XXX v People ponente)
Jose Catral Mendoza (Justice; Imbong v Ochoa ponente)
Hilario G. Davide Jr. (constitutional framer; later CJ)
institutions: Supreme Court of the Philippines (en banc)
Second Plenary Council of the Philippines (PCP2)
St. Paul's Superstore (San Antonio Village, Makati)
cases: XXX v People (April 16, 2024) â€” J Hernando, en banc, 9-5-1
Imbong v Ochoa (April 9, 2014) â€” J Mendoza, en banc, 11-1-3, 106 pages
laws_treaties: Republic Act No. 9262 (Anti-Violence Against Women and Their Children Act of 2004), Sec. 5(i)
1987 Philippine Constitution (right to life from conception)
events: PCP2 (Second Plenary Council of the Philippines) â€” implementation of Vatican II in PH

stances.claim: Picking up a reader's keen observation that last Monday's column promised a 'rocky road' but only gave easy examples (15-0 and 13-2), here is a genuinely rocky one: XXX v People (April 16, 2024, J Hernando, en banc, 9-5-1) holding that marital infidelity causing mental or emotional anguish is psychological violence punishable under RA 9262.

The majority's key move was to hold that the specific intent to cause anguish 'may be conclusively presumed from the fact of infidelity itself' â€” even one casual or infrequent encounter qualifies â€” while the minority of five contended that criminal intent had not been proven and is looking for another similar case when the Court's composition changes.

CJ's 'simple advice to husbands' is that infidelity, casual or occasional, with willing mistress or one-night stand, is dangerous to life and liberty â€” though as to wives, RA 9262 is sub-silentio: it punishes only philandering husbands.

The alleged right to abortion has become a hot US presidential-campaign issue, but in the Philippines such a battle is passÃ© â€” abortion is barred by the Constitution and penalized by law; the only remaining question is when life begins.

Imbong v Ochoa (April 9, 2014, J Mendoza, en banc, 11-1-3) definitively ruled that life begins at fertilization â€” the sperm-egg union â€” and rejected Rep. Edcel Lagman's implantation theory; the constitutional framers (especially Villegas and Davide) had stressed life must be protected 'from the moment of conception.'

Every human being enjoys the right to life â€” 'inherent and not a creation of, or dependent upon a particular law, custom, or belief' â€” so health professionals cannot be forced to use post-fertilization devices/drugs, and some theologians prescribe only the 'rhythm method' in family planning.

PCP2 Article 28 instructs lay men and women in responsible position to help form the civic conscience and promote leaders of true integrity, while bishops, priests, and religious must refrain from partisan politics â€” especially the use of the pulpit for partisan purposes â€” per Canons 287 #2 and 672.

notable_anecdotes.summary: The reader-prompted opening â€” 'A reader observedâ€¦ What a keen observation!' â€” modeling responsiveness and using the reader as the engine of the column's structure.
The gender-asymmetry observation â€” 'RA 9262 punishes only philandering husbands; it is sub-silentio on philandering wives' â€” quietly noting the statute's protective-but-one-sided design.

The 'simple advice to husbands' aphorism â€” 'Infidelity, though casual or occasional, and whether with a willing mistress or a one-night stand, is dangerous to life and liberty' â€” turning doctrine into accessible personal counsel.
Naming Justices Lazaro-Javier and Singh as 'incumbent lady Justices' who wrote compelling concurrences â€” a careful gendered notice that the female bench voted with the majority.

The 'composition of the Court changes' note â€” the minority's openly stated strategy of waiting for personnel turnover to revisit the holding â€” a rare moment when CJ surfaces SC strategic litigation dynamics.

The Imbong-Lagman naming â€” 'the Court cannot subscribe to the theory advocated by Hon. [Edcel] Lagman that life begins at implantation' â€” preserving the named-individual disagreement at the doctrinal level.

The book-purchase appendix close â€” 'purchase the book for P308 (paperback edition) from St. Paul's Superstore in San Antonio Village, Makati' â€” extremely concrete consumer guidance for further canonical reading.

decision_framework_signals: applies the conclusively-presumed-intent doctrine for psychological violence under RA 9262
applies the life-begins-at-fertilization constitutional doctrine (Imbong v Ochoa) as the operative bioethics floor
uses framers-record corroboration (Villegas, Davide on 'moment of conception') as interpretive backing
uses the comparative-civics framing (US live issue vs PH settled) to defuse imported controversies
applies the PCP2 Article 28 division of labor (laity-political / clergy-non-partisan) as the operative Church-state interface
treats the minority's 'await composition change' strategy as surfaced doctrinal contestation
uses canonical-text quotation as authoritative basis

Members of the Philippine bench and bar

one_paragraph_summary: A reader observed that the prior Monday's column called the road to liberalism of the Supreme Court and the Catholic Church 'rocky' yet gave only easy examples (15-0 and 13-2). CJ accepts the correction and offers a genuinely rocky case: XXX v People (April 16, 2024, J Hernando, en banc, 9-5-1) ruled that marital infidelity causing mental or emotional anguish is psychological violence punishable with imprisonment and fine under Section 5(i) of RA 9262. The majority held that the specific intent to cause anguish 'may be conclusively presumed from the fact of infidelity itself' â€” even casual or infrequent â€” with Justices Lazaro-Javier and Singh writing compelling concurrences. The minority of five contended that criminal intent had not been proven and is looking for another similar case to prevail when the Court's composition changes. CJ's simple advice to husbands: infidelity is 'dangerous to life and liberty' â€” though RA 9262 punishes only philandering husbands and is sub-silentio on philandering wives. On abortion, hot in the US presidential campaign, CJ notes the battle is passÃ© in the Philippines because abortion is barred by the Constitution and criminalized by law; the only question is when life begins. Imbong v Ochoa (April 9, 2014, J Mendoza, en banc, 11-1-3, 106 pages) ruled that 'a zygote is a human organism' and 'the life of a new human being commencesâ€¦ upon fertilization,' explicitly rejecting Rep. Edcel Lagman's implantation theory; constitutional framers Bernardo M. Villegas and (later CJ) Hilario G. Davide Jr. had stressed protection 'from the moment of conception,' and the right to life is 'grounded on natural law,' inherent and not a creation of statute. Consequently, health professionals cannot be forced to use post-fertilization devices or drugs; some theologians prescribe only the 'rhythm method.' On PCP2, CJ disclaims canon-law expertise but quotes Article 28: lay men and women in responsible positions must help form the civic conscience and promote leaders of true integrity, while bishops, priests, and religious must refrain from partisan politics â€” especially from using the pulpit for partisan purposes â€” per Canons 287 #2 and 672. Further questions, he says, may be addressed to a canon lawyer or by buying the P308 paperback of the 'Acts and Decrees' from St. Paul's Superstore in San Antonio Village, Makati.
